app_goal: Realtime multi-speaker speech-to-English translation with same-voice, same-volume playback and source-voice suppression.

preferred: model cards or official implementation docs for runnable candidates
allowed_with_label: blog posts used only for lead discovery
required_for_decision: at least one primary source per candidate
known failure modes and detractor concerns
a disposable benchmark or smoke-test plan

detractor_loop.purpose: Force every attractive candidate through a skeptic pass before it becomes implementation work.
required_questions: What practical assumption would make this recommendation fail in a real noisy room?
What part of the result is from a peer-reviewed source, and what part is only a preprint, vendor claim, or demo?
What latency, hardware, licensing, privacy, or safety constraint could make the option unusable?
What benchmark would disprove the recommendation quickly and cheaply?
What fallback should the app use when the candidate fails open?
minimum_output: one condition that would reverse the decision

questions.decision: Choose the live capture, VAD, denoising, and level-estimation front end.
questions.implementation_surface: future capture service plus crates/audio_core loudness metadata
questions.robin_task: Find the best current real-time microphone capture, VAD, denoising, and loudness-estimation approach for noisy rooms with multiple speakers and overlapping speech.
queries: real-time speech enhancement noise suppression voice activity detection noisy multi speaker rooms 2025 peer reviewed
streaming VAD speech enhancement real-time factor noisy conversational speech benchmark
speech loudness normalization dBFS LUFS perceived volume matching real-time speech playback
questions.benchmark_fixture: synthetic room clips with calibrated speech/noise levels and at least one live microphone smoke test
questions.acceptance_gate: Candidate must estimate speech presence and input level without blocking the downstream diarization path.
questions.detractor_focus: Reject candidates that only work on clean close-talk speech or require calibration too fragile for a moving room.

questions.decision: Choose the online diarization and speaker-tracking path for overlapping speakers.
questions.implementation_surface: gateway diarization adapter, proto speaker events, Flutter speaker lanes
questions.robin_task: Compare streaming diarization methods for overlapping speakers, especially online Sortformer, EEND-family systems, pyannote-derived pipelines, and speaker-attributed ASR options.
queries: streaming speaker diarization overlapping speech Sortformer EEND Interspeech 2025
online speaker diarization overlapping conversational speech DER JER benchmark
speaker attributed ASR diarization overlapping speech streaming benchmark
questions.benchmark_fixture: AMI or AliMeeting-style overlapping clips plus a local two-person overlap fixture
questions.acceptance_gate: Candidate must produce stable speaker ids and overlapping_speaker_ids compatible with the current session contract.
questions.detractor_focus: Challenge any claim that speaker labels stay stable under overlap, speaker re-entry, unknown speaker count, and low-latency chunks.

questions.decision: Choose blind separation or target-speaker extraction for overlapping speech before ASR/TTS.
questions.implementation_surface: future Rust/Python audio separation module and gateway audio metadata
questions.robin_task: Find efficient speech separation or target-speaker extraction models that can run with low latency and preserve ASR-relevant intelligibility in reverberant noisy rooms.
queries: real-time neural speech separation overlapping speakers low latency ICLR 2025 TIGER TF-GridNet
target speaker extraction streaming speech separation real-time benchmark 2025
tiny real-time neural speech separation hearable devices TF-MLPNet 2025
questions.benchmark_fixture: LibriCSS/LibriMix-style mixtures plus captured two-speaker local mixtures
questions.acceptance_gate: Candidate must improve downstream ASR or diarization enough to justify added latency.
questions.detractor_focus: Treat separation as optional until it proves downstream ASR or diarization gains exceed added artifacts and delay.

questions.decision: Choose language identification, streaming ASR, and into-English translation architecture.
questions.implementation_surface: gateway translation/audio provider adapters and proto detected_language fields
questions.robin_task: Compare multilingual ASR plus translation approaches for source-language detection and low-latency English output, including Whisper-family, SeamlessM4T, and current streaming speech translation systems.
queries: multilingual streaming ASR language identification speech translation into English benchmark 2025
Whisper multilingual speech recognition language identification limitations noisy speech peer reviewed
SeamlessM4T speech to text translation robustness background noise speaker variation Nature 2025
questions.benchmark_fixture: FLEURS/CoVoST2 plus local mixed-language room clips
questions.acceptance_gate: Candidate must expose language confidence and translated text early enough for same-turn playback.
questions.detractor_focus: Punish models that look strong offline but cannot stream partials, recover from wrong language ID, or handle code-switching.

questions.decision: Decide whether the product runtime should be cascaded ASR+MT+TTS, direct speech-to-speech, or a hybrid.
questions.implementation_surface: gateway orchestration and future audio stream contracts
questions.robin_task: Evaluate direct speech-to-speech translation versus cascaded ASR, text translation, and voice-conditioned TTS for a realtime same-voice English playback app.
queries: direct speech to speech translation versus cascaded ASR MT TTS latency quality 2025
streaming speech translation speaker diarization overlapping speech DiariST ICASSP 2024
multimodal language model direct speech-to-speech translation streaming vocoder 2025
questions.benchmark_fixture: one cascaded baseline and one direct/hybrid baseline on identical multilingual room clips
questions.acceptance_gate: Decision record must justify why the chosen architecture best preserves real-time behavior and same-voice output.
questions.detractor_focus: Assume direct S2ST demos hide debuggability, voice-clone control, and source-suppression integration costs until proven otherwise.

questions.decision: Choose the same-speaker English output strategy.
questions.robin_task: Compare zero-shot voice cloning, voice conversion, and speaker-conditioned TTS for fast English output that matches source speaker identity without unsafe persistence.
queries: zero shot voice cloning TTS few seconds reference speaker similarity MOS 2025 peer reviewed
streaming voice conversion target speaker extraction voice cloning low latency 2025
speaker adaptive text to speech denoising diffusion GAN zero-shot Scientific Reports 2025
questions.benchmark_fixture: short consented speaker references with generated English prompts and blind similarity scoring
questions.acceptance_gate: Candidate must support ephemeral references, visible readiness states, and fallback voice behavior.
questions.detractor_focus: Reject voice cloning paths that cannot prove consent-safe reference handling, bounded latency, and acceptable cross-language speaker similarity.

questions.decision: Choose the translated playback mixer and loudness-matching strategy.
questions.implementation_surface: crates/audio_core, Flutter playback controls, future platform audio output
questions.robin_task: Find practical low-latency speech mixing, gain staging, loudness matching, and clipping prevention methods for translated speech playback at the source speaker's perceived volume.
queries: real-time speech loudness matching dBFS LUFS playback mixer low latency
speech audio gain normalization clipping prevention conversational playback
perceived loudness matching speech synthesis original speaker volume
questions.benchmark_fixture: fixed loudness source clips with translated playback rendered at matching levels
questions.acceptance_gate: Candidate must map to input_level_dbfs and output_level_dbfs without unstable gain jumps.
questions.detractor_focus: Challenge perceived-volume claims with clipping, distance changes, whisper/shout extremes, and multi-speaker simultaneous playback.

questions.decision: Choose the original-source suppression or cancellation approach.
questions.implementation_surface: future DSP module, gateway suppression metadata, Flutter diagnostics
questions.robin_task: Compare source-voice suppression, echo cancellation, target speech suppression, and translated playback masking approaches for real-time use without corrupting translated output.
queries: real-time speech source suppression target speaker cancellation overlapping speech 2025
acoustic echo cancellation translated speech playback microphone suppression low latency
speech enhancement target speech removal source separation cancellation benchmark
questions.benchmark_fixture: same-room capture/playback loop with known source and translated reference tracks
questions.acceptance_gate: Candidate must report original_voice_suppression_db and fail open when cancellation is unreliable.
questions.detractor_focus: Assume single-device source-voice cancellation is physically limited; require a masking/fail-open fallback and hardware assumptions.

questions.decision: Choose the end-to-end benchmark suite and target hardware profile.
questions.robin_task: Define a benchmark suite that measures the whole realtime loop from capture to English playback across overlapping speakers, language changes, volume changes, and noisy rooms.
queries: benchmark realtime speech translation diarization overlapping speakers latency evaluation
LibriCSS AMI AliMeeting CHiME FLEURS CoVoST2 speech translation diarization evaluation
edge deployment speech translation diarization TTS real-time GPU CPU benchmark
questions.benchmark_fixture: a versioned local fixture pack plus optional public benchmark subsets
questions.acceptance_gate: Every provider/model integration must add a disposable smoke check and at least one benchmark fixture.
questions.detractor_focus: Reject benchmark suites that only measure isolated model quality and ignore end-to-end latency, privacy, source suppression, and degraded-room failure.
